name: coda-automation
description: Automate Coda tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): manage docs, pages, tables, rows, formulas, permissions, and publishing. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Search and browse Coda documents by query or list all available docs
Resolve Coda URLs to document, page, and table IDs automatically
List, read, and write table rows with upsert operations
Manage document permissions and sharing settings
Export documents to HTML or Markdown format
Publish and unpublish documents with custom domains
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 77 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is a documentation/guide for Coda MCP operations. External command patterns are markdown code formatting, network is the legitimate rube.app endpoint, weak crypto and system reconnaissance are scanner misinterpretations of documentation text. Safe to publish.
valueStatement: Managing Coda documents manually takes time and effort. This skill provides automated workflows for searching documents, managing tables, rows, formulas, permissions, and publishing via the Rube MCP server.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server to be configured and connected
Requires active Coda connection via the Composio platform
Cannot modify document structure (add/remove tables, columns)

useCases.targetUser: Team leads and project managers who use Coda for documentation
useCases.title: Automate Document Workflows
useCases.description: Search and manage Coda documents programmatically, reducing manual document handling

useCases.targetUser: Developers and data analysts integrating Coda with other systems
useCases.title: Data Synchronization
useCases.description: Sync external data with Coda tables using upsert operations

useCases.targetUser: Content creators and teams sharing documentation externally
useCases.title: Document Publishing
useCases.description: Publish Coda documents publicly or manage access permissions

promptTemplates.title: Find a Document
promptTemplates.prompt: Search for Coda documents containing the term 'project timeline' and list the first 5 results with their IDs.

promptTemplates.title: Get Table Data
promptTemplates.prompt: List all tables in document 'AbCdEfGhIj', then list all rows from the 'Tasks' table with column names.

promptTemplates.title: Upsert Data
promptTemplates.prompt: Upsert a row into table 'Employees' in document 'AbCdEfGhIj' with keyColumn 'email'. Row data: name='John Doe', email='john@example.com', department='Engineering'.

promptTemplates.title: Publish Document
promptTemplates.prompt: Publish document 'AbCdEfGhIj' with slug 'my-public-doc' and get the sharing metadata to verify access settings.

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get the current tool schemas as they may change
Use CODA_RESOLVE_BROWSER_LINK to convert Coda URLs to IDs rather than manually extracting them
Use keyColumns with unique values for reliable upsert operations
antiPatterns: Do not assume table or column IDs are stable - always list them first
Do not skip checking connection status before running workflows
Do not forget to handle rate limits - implement backoff for 429 responses

faq.question: How do I connect to Coda?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server, then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'coda' to authenticate.

faq.question: What is the difference between table ID and table name?
faq.answer: Table IDs are system identifiers like 'grid-abc', while table names are human-readable. Use CODA_LIST_TABLES to get IDs.

faq.question: How does upsert work?
faq.answer: Upsert inserts a row if no match exists on keyColumns, or updates the row if a match is found.

faq.question: Can I export entire documents?
faq.answer: Yes, use CODA_BEGIN_CONTENT_EXPORT with outputFormat 'html' or 'markdown' for full or partial exports.

faq.question: What permission levels are available?
faq.answer: Three levels: readonly (view only), write (edit), and comment (can add comments).

faq.question: How do I handle rate limits?
faq.answer: Implement exponential backoff on 429 responses. Use bulk operations via CODA_UPSERT_ROWS instead of individual updates.
